mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate has been pinned near zero (0.16% as of Feb 2011) for over two years, sustaining a low discount-rate environment that supports equity valuations. As long as FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, the persistent liquidity subsidy mechanically compresses the equity risk premium and produces positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window during the ZIRP period, or positive-return frequency in ZIRP months is not statistically greater than the full-sample base rate (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals labor market healing that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month. UNRATE fell from 9.8% (Nov 2010) to 9.0% (Jan 2011 revised), a decline exceeding this threshold.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines more than 0.3pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: In periods where the UNRATE decline trigger fires, subsequent 20d forward returns are negative in more than 45% of instances, or the effect disappears when tested against a matched sample of non-triggered periods (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in INDPRO (industrial production) and RSAFS (retail sales) signals synchronized supply-side and demand-side expansion, a stronger confirmation of broad economic momentum than either series alone. When both series show positive month-over-month growth simultaneously, the combined signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter, exceeding the predictive power of INDPRO or RSAFS individually.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both show positive MoM growth in the same month
failure_condition: The joint-signal Rank IC does not exceed the maximum of the two individual single-series Rank ICs by at least 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-financial-crisis recovery regime with depressed housing starts (HOUST at 630K, far below the ~1,500K pre-crisis norm), any sustained uptick in HOUST signals renewed confidence in household balance sheets and construction-linked demand, translating into positive equity returns over the following quarter. This mechanism is regime-specific and should not hold in normal-cycle periods where housing starts are already near trend.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 2 consecutive months from a depressed base below 700K
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage_value=596.0 revised=630.0, far below historical norms
failure_condition: During periods where HOUST rises from a depressed base, 60d forward returns are not significantly different from unconditional base rate returns (p > 0.10), or the effect reverses sign in more than one out of three tested historical recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Low and stable CPIAUCSL inflation (around 221 index level, modest month-over-month growth) removes near-term pressure on the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, reducing rate-hike risk premia embedded in short-horizon equity pricing and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward return Rank IC with CPIAUCSL level or trend is below 0.02 across the full sample, or the hypothesis fails to outperform a null random-walk benchmark in a paired t-test (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When monthly PAYEMS net change exceeds 150,000 jobs added, it signals a labor market inflection strong enough to support consumption-driven earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month. PAYEMS rose from 130,515K to 131,057K (revised) in early 2011, approaching this threshold as recovery gains momentum.
failure_condition: In months where the PAYEMS threshold triggers, forward 20d returns are positive in fewer than 55% of instances, or revisions to PAYEMS reverse the trigger condition in more than 30% of cases when using real-time vintage data.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent positive month-over-month trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial capacity utilization and forward-looking corporate output plans, which is continuously priced into equities and produces a small but consistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the following week regardless of regime.
failure_condition: The sign concordance between INDPRO MoM trend direction and 5d forward SPX returns is not statistically distinguishable from 50% (binomial test p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains near zero while UNRATE is simultaneously declining, the combination signals that monetary policy is deliberately staying accommodative even as labor conditions improve, an implicit policy put that amplifies risk-taking in equities. This joint condition (FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and UNRATE falling) produces a stronger positive quarterly return signal than either series alone, but the effect is expected to reverse once the Fed begins signaling liftoff.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.5% AND UNRATE declines for 3 consecutive months
regime_hint: ZIRP combined with improving labor market (dovish policy put regime)
failure_condition: The joint-trigger 60d return distribution is not significantly more positive than the distribution conditioned on UNRATE decline alone (Mann-Whitney p > 0.10), indicating no genuine interaction beyond the labor signal; or the effect fails to reverse within 6 months of the first Fed liftoff signal post-2011.
